section.heading: Application of certain criminal laws to acts on aircraft
section.chapeau: An individual on an aircraft in the special aircraft jurisdiction of the United States who commits an act that—

paragraph.content: if committed in the special maritime and territorial jurisdiction of the United States (as defined in section 7 of title 18) would violate section 113, 114, 661, 662, 1111, 1112, 1113, or 2111 or chapter 109A of title 18, shall be fined under title 18, imprisoned under that section or chapter, or both; or
if committed in the District of Columbia would violate section 9 of the Act of July 29, 1892 (D.C. Code § 22-1112), shall be fined under title 18, imprisoned under section 9 of the Act, or both.

html:tr.style: font-size:6pt; border-bottom:1px solid black; border-top:1px solid black; -uslm-lc:h1;

html:th.style: min-width: 44.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-right:1px solid black;
width:68.0pt ; max-width:68.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-right:1px solid black; border-left:1px solid black;
width:94.0pt ; max-width:94.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-left:1px solid black;

html:td.style: text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-right:1px solid black; padding-right:2pt;
text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-right:1px solid black; border-left:1px solid black; padding-right:2pt; padding-left: 2pt;
text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-left:1px solid black; padding-left: 2pt;

note.p: In clause (1), the words “fined under title 18, imprisoned under that section or chapter, or both” are substituted for “punished as provided therein” for consistency with title 18.
In clause (2), the words “fined under title 18, imprisoned under section 9 of the Act, or both” are substituted for “punished as provided therein” for consistency with title 18.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: Section 9 of the Act of July 29, 1892, referred to in par. (2), is section 9 of act July 29, 1892, ch. 320, 27 Stat. 324, which is not classified to the Code. Section 9 of the Act was reclassified to section 22–1312 of the D.C. Code (2014).
